title: ferocactus diguetii subsp. carmenensis
intro: Ferocactus diguetii subsp. carmenensis, also known as the Carmen barrel cactus, is a rare and endemic species of cactus native to the state of Sonora in Mexico. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ferocactus diguetii subsp. carmenensis include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the stems in water or oil, and it can also be used as a powder to create topical creams and ointments.
